title: aframmi angolense
intro: Aframmi angolense, also known as African Ginger or White Ginger, is a perennial plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). The plant's roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Aframmi angolense are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, making it beneficial for treating various health issues, such as arthritis and fever. In traditional medicine, the plant's roots are often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or dried and used as a powder for herbal teas, and capsules, to harness its health benefits.
